name: GLUT2 (glucose transporter 2)
function: Low-affinity facilitative glucose uniporter; bidirectional glucose flux across the sinusoidal membrane so the hepatocyte takes up glucose when blood is high and releases it (after gluconeogenesis/glycogenolysis) when blood is low.
topology: 12-TM integral major-facilitator-superfamily carrier (two 6-TM bundles); both N- and C-termini cytosolic; large extracellular loop between TM1 and TM2.
citation: Jumper et al., Nature 2021, 596:583, DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2; Varadi et al., Nucleic Acids Res 2024, 52:D368, DOI 10.1093/nar/gkad1011 (AlphaFold DB).
note: PREDICTED model AF-P11168-F1 (model_v6, global pLDDT 86.62) - no experimental human GLUT2 structure exists in the PDB (experimental SLC2A relatives: GLUT1 4PYP/6THA, GLUT3, GLUT4 7WSN). Orientation NOT calibrated: renderer must embed the 12-TM bundle in the bilayer (termini cytosolic) and may borrow tilt from an OPM-oriented GLUT homolog.

name: Na+/K+-ATPase (alpha1/beta1 sodium-potassium pump)
function: P-type ATPase that hydrolyses ATP to export 3 Na+ and import 2 K+ per cycle, building the Na+ electrochemical gradient and resting membrane potential that power secondary-active basolateral uptake (e.g. NTCP bile-salt cotransport).
topology: Multi-pass integral P-type ATPase: alpha1 subunit ~10 TM helices with a large cytosolic head (Actuator, Nucleotide-binding, Phosphorylation domains); single-TM beta1 subunit (glycosylated ectodomain extracellular) and FXYD regulatory peptide. Big soluble catalytic head is cytosolic.
citation: Guo, Zhang, Yan, Huang, Ye, Wu, Chi, Shi, Zhou, 'Cryo-EM structures of recombinant human sodium-potassium pump determined in three different states', Nat Commun 2022, 13:3957, DOI 10.1038/s41467-022-31602-y, PMID 35803952.
note: Experimental human alpha1/beta1 pump in the E1.3Na state (cytosolic gate open, 3 Na+ bound). OPM-oriented file (bilayer half-thickness 15.3 A, ~31 A membrane). Extracellular = +z derived here: large cytosolic catalytic head sits at -z (mean CA z = -19).

name: NTCP (Na+/taurocholate cotransporting polypeptide)
function: Na+-coupled secondary-active uptake of conjugated bile salts (e.g. taurocholate) from sinusoidal blood into the hepatocyte - the major basolateral step of the enterohepatic bile-salt circulation; also the hepatitis B/D virus receptor.
topology: Integral SLC10-family carrier, ~9-10 TM helices with a core/panel two-domain fold; extracellular N-terminus, cytosolic C-terminus.
citation: Goutam, Ielasi, Pardon, Steyaert, Reyes, 'Structural basis of sodium-dependent bile salt uptake into the liver', Nature 2022, 606:1015, DOI 10.1038/s41586-022-04723-z, PMID 35545671.
note: Experimental human NTCP, but a THERMOSTABILISED construct with a conformation-locking nanobody (Nb87) bound; apo (no bile salt). OPM-oriented (bilayer half-thickness 15.6 A). Extracellular = +z: NTCP chain A is centred in the bilayer (mean CA z ~0) and Nb87 (chain B, mean CA z = -39) decorates the cytosolic face, confirming -z is cytosolic. Nanobody chain should be hidden/ignored by the renderer.

name: BSEP (bile salt export pump)
function: ATP-driven primary-active export of monovalent conjugated bile salts across the canalicular (apical) membrane into bile - the rate-limiting step of bile-salt secretion; loss causes PFIC2 cholestasis (the engine's cholestasis demo).
topology: ABCB-family full ABC exporter: 12 TM helices (two 6-TM bundles) forming the translocation pathway, plus two cytosolic nucleotide-binding domains (NBDs) that bind/hydrolyse ATP. Substrate exits to the extracellular/canalicular side; NBDs cytosolic.
citation: Wang, Hou, Chen, Yang, Wen, Chen, Hao, Zhou, 'Cryo-EM structure of human bile salts exporter ABCB11', Cell Res 2020, 30:623, DOI 10.1038/s41422-020-0302-0, PMID 32203132.
note: Experimental human BSEP, apo, inward-open (no bile salt / nucleotide bound). OPM-oriented (bilayer half-thickness 15.5 A). Extracellular/canalicular lumen = +z: the two cytosolic NBDs sit strongly at -z (mean CA z = -34).

name: MRP2 (multidrug resistance-associated protein 2)
function: ATP-driven canalicular export of conjugated organic anions - chiefly bilirubin glucuronides/conjugates and glutathione/glucuronide/sulfate-conjugated xenobiotics - into bile; loss causes Dubin-Johnson syndrome (conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia).
topology: ABCC-family ABC exporter with an extra N-terminal TMD0 (5 TM) plus the core 12-TM exporter (two 6-TM bundles) and two cytosolic NBDs; a regulatory (R) domain is inserted between the two half-modules. NBDs and R-domain cytosolic; substrate exits to canaliculus.
citation: Mao, Chen, Wang, Wang, Zhou, Hou, Chen, 'Transport mechanism of human bilirubin transporter ABCC2 tuned by the inter-module regulatory domain', Nat Commun 2024, 15:1061, DOI 10.1038/s41467-024-45337-5, PMID 38316776.
note: Experimental human MRP2 with a CONJUGATED-BILIRUBIN substrate (bilirubin ditaurate) bound - functionally ideal. NOT in OPM: orientation is the raw deposited cryo-EM frame and is NOT membrane-calibrated, so the renderer must orient it (membrane-spanning TMDs, NBDs + R-domain cytosolic). Tilt can be approximated from the OPM-oriented ABC exporter BSEP (6LR0, same fold) - FLAGGED as approximate.

name: Glucokinase (hexokinase IV, GCK)
function: Phosphorylates glucose to glucose-6-phosphate (EC 2.7.1.1); the cytosolic high-S0.5 sigmoidal glucose sensor that commits hepatic glucose to glycolysis/glycogen only when blood glucose is high.
citation: Kamata, Mitsuya, Nishimura, Eiki, Nagata, 'Structural Basis for Allosteric Regulation of the Monomeric Allosteric Enzyme Human Glucokinase', Structure 2004, 12:429, DOI 10.1016/j.str.2004.02.005, PMID 15016359.
note: Pre-existing file (public/glucokinase.pdb), NOT re-downloaded. Experimental human GCK in the closed/active conformation captured WITH a non-physiological synthetic activator (MRK); modelled residues 14-461 (N-terminal ~13 + disordered residues absent). The engine already models GCK kinetics (S0.5 ~8 mM, Hill ~1.7).

name: CPS1 (carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase 1)
function: Mitochondrial-matrix enzyme that condenses ammonia + bicarbonate + 2 ATP into carbamoyl phosphate (EC 6.3.4.16), the committed first step of the urea cycle; N-acetylglutamate (NAG) is its essential allosteric activator (the urea-cycle on/off switch).
topology: Soluble mitochondrial-matrix multidomain enzyme (~1500 residues); not membrane-integral.
citation: de Cima, Polo, Diez-Fernandez, Aguado-Llera, Cervera, Fita, Rubio, 'Structure of human carbamoyl phosphate synthetase: deciphering the on/off switch of human ureagenesis', Sci Rep 2015, 5:16950, DOI 10.1038/srep16950, PMID 26592762.
note: Experimental human CPS1, ligand-bound ACTIVE form (NAG activator + ADP/phosphate engaged). Large soluble matrix enzyme - place inside the mitochondrial matrix, not in a membrane. Grounds the urea-cycle entry the engine models.
